acceptance_targets: [ch04-booker-001] PASS if revised material between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:202 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:320 contains no more than two refusal cycles and each cycle introduces a distinct new leverage change visible on-page; FAIL if a third cycle repeats 'no amount/no transfer' without a new irreversible state change.

[ch04-craft-001] PASS if revised spans around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:238-245 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:286-314 include at least one concrete, observable consequence event (not just projected probabilities or generic promises) and keep the chapter's decision climax grounded in scene; FAIL if the beat remains primarily abstract accounting plus summary language.

[ch04-craft-002] PASS if each revised refusal beat in the lines anchored above contains one direct self-assessment sentence and one physical marker of strain tied to that admission; FAIL if refusals remain only logistical phrasing with no explicit internal stake naming.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised material between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:202 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:320 contains no more than two refusal cycles and each cycle introduces a distinct new leverage change visible on-page; FAIL if a third cycle repeats 'no amount/no transfer' without a new irreversible state change.
findings.problem: The chapter replays the same refusal beat (attempt, delay, no amount committed) across multiple cycles without enough new causal movement, so escalation feels looped rather than sharpened.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten one of the repeated refusal loops in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:202 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:250, then spend reclaimed space on a new irreversible consequence anchored in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:286 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:318 (e.g., a concrete account-state change, filing step, or locked-out option that cannot be undone by another promise text).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised spans around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:238-245 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:286-314 include at least one concrete, observable consequence event (not just projected probabilities or generic promises) and keep the chapter's decision climax grounded in scene; FAIL if the beat remains primarily abstract accounting plus summary language.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: at the key harm window, the prose retreats into abstract planning language and probability bookkeeping, which cushions the immediate family consequence instead of forcing a present-tense moral collision.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:238 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:245, replace at least one abstract ledger/probability block with a concrete in-scene consequence beat tied to June/Nora's exposure; in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:286 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:312, swap summary phrasing for embodied action/reaction that shows the cost of his delay in the moment.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each revised refusal beat in the lines anchored above contains one direct self-assessment sentence and one physical marker of strain tied to that admission; FAIL if refusals remain only logistical phrasing with no explicit internal stake naming.
findings.problem: Timidity at decision beats: Eli's refusals stay in managerial euphemism ('full hit', 'start sending this week') rather than exposing the sharper internal motive he is protecting.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:266, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:288, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:320, add one explicit internal line naming what he chooses to preserve (mobility/control) at whose expense, paired with immediate bodily response rather than administrative phrasing.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
